description: Terms found in incident reports but potentially missing from the glossary. Queued for weekly glossary-term-updater.

candidates.context: Solana Foundation STRIDE program (Security Threat Response, Investigation, and Detection Engagement) - referenced in Drift Protocol exploit report
candidates.suggestedDefinition: Security Threat Response, Investigation, and Detection Engagement — a Solana Foundation initiative providing security evaluations and threat monitoring for DeFi protocols

candidates.context: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) weaponized for token theft — core technique in EvilTokens PhaaS campaign
candidates.suggestedDefinition: A phishing technique that abuses the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to intercept authentication tokens, routing victims through legitimate MFA challenges while attackers capture the resulting tokens

candidates.context: Attack mechanism used in Drift Protocol exploit to bypass multisig governance on Solana blockchain
candidates.suggestedDefinition: An attack technique exploiting Solana's durable nonce feature to pre-sign transactions that remain valid indefinitely, enabling attackers to bypass time-based security controls in multisig governance

candidates.context: Vulnerability exploited in LexisNexis AWS breach — enables remote code execution via React frontend
candidates.suggestedDefinition: A critical vulnerability in React-based frontend applications that allows remote attackers to achieve shell access through crafted requests, enabling unauthorized access to backend cloud infrastructure

candidates.context: FBI court-authorized operation to disrupt APT28's DNS hijacking infrastructure by resetting compromised router DNS settings
candidates.suggestedDefinition: A 2026 FBI law enforcement operation that disrupted Russian APT28's DNS hijacking network by performing court-authorized DNS resets on thousands of compromised SOHO routers across 120+ countries

candidates.context: Phishing-as-a-Service model used by EvilTokens platform to distribute device code phishing kits via Telegram
candidates.suggestedDefinition: Phishing-as-a-Service — a cybercrime business model where operators provide turnkey phishing kits, infrastructure, and support to customers for a fee, lowering the barrier to entry for credential theft campaigns

candidates.context: Electronic Patient Dossier — Dutch healthcare record system used by ChipSoft
Netherlands healthcare computer emergency response team
Chrome API for CSS font feature values, exploited in CVE-2026-2441
Technique where a legitimate application loads a malicious DLL
Electronic Health Record system by Epic Systems, disrupted in UMMC attack
